label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the order of the books on the shelf. There are five books: white, green, brown, gray, and orange. The order is fixed, and we have some clues to work with. Let me list out the clues again to make sure I don't miss any. First, the gray book is to the right of the orange book. So wherever the orange book is, the gray book must be somewhere to its right. That means orange can't be the rightmost book because there's no space to the right of that. Similarly, gray can't be the leftmost book if orange is to the left of it. Second, the green book is the second from the right. Since there are five books, the positions can be numbered from left to right as 1 to 5. The second from the right would be position 4 (since rightmost is 5, so second from right is 4). So green is in position 4. Third, the brown book is to the right of the white book. So white is somewhere to the left of brown. And fourth, the brown book is to the left of the orange book. So brown is to the left of orange. So putting those two together: white < brown < orange. So white is left of brown, and brown is left of orange, so white is left of brown, which is left of orange. So we have white, then brown, then orange somewhere. Also, gray is to the right of orange, so after orange comes gray. Let me try to map out the positions. Positions: 1 2 3 4 5 (left to right)
